channel.title: Dfir-Ogre documentation – Registry
channel.description: Recent content in Registry on Dfir-Ogre documentation

item.description:
High‑level description

The Ac Mru plug‑in extracts Search Assistant entries from user’s registry hive. .

  • Retrieves search queries entered in the Windows Search Assistant.
  • Provides the chronological order of the queries via order_index.

File: configuration/acmru.xml
Plugin parser: RegAcMru (implemented in src/anssi_ogre_plugin/registry/acmru.py)

Timeline data

The timeline displays the following elements:

Timeline field Value Description
Description search_request The search query string stored by the Search Assistant.
Additional Description
Related User key_security.owner_sid SID of the user who owns the registry key.

High‑level description

The AmCache Driver plug‑in extracts driver metadata stored in the AmCache hive.

  • Retrieves driver attributes (name, version, product, company, etc.).
  • Captures file‑system information (path, size, SHA‑1, compilation date).

File: configuration/registry/amcache_driver.xml
Plugin parser: RegAmCacheDriver – implemented in src/anssi_ogre_plugin/registry/amcache_driver.py

Timeline data

The timeline displays the following elements:

Timeline field Value Description
Description name Driver name (DriverName).
Additional Description path File system path of the driver binary.
size Size of the driver file in bytes.
service Service name associated with the driver.
key_path Registry key path of the entry.
sha1 SHA‑1 hash of the driver binary (from key name or DriverId).
Related User key_security.owner_sid SID of the user who owns the registry key.

High‑level description

The files plug‑in Retrieves cached executable file metadata from the Windows AmCache registry hive,

  • Expose what the OS stores for quick access to recently used files.
  • Extracts file name, path, size, SHA‑1 hash, program identifiers, version info, timestamps, and security descriptors for forensic examination.

File: configuration/registry/amcache_files.xml
Plugin parser: RegAmCacheFile – implemented in src/anssi_ogre_plugin/registry/amcache_files.py

Timeline data

The timeline display the following elements:

Timeline field Value Description
Description path Full file path of the cached entry.
Related user key_security.owner_sid Owner SID of the registry key.
Additional description sha1 SHA‑1 hash of the file (FileId).
size Size of the file in bytes.
program_id Identifier of the associated program (APP_ID).
key_path Registry key path where the entry resides.

High‑level description

The AmCache Program plug‑in extracts metadata about installed programs from the Windows AmCache hive.

  • Retrieves program identifiers, names, versions, and publishers.
  • Captures installation source, directory paths, and MSI product/package codes.

File: configuration/registry/amcache_program.xml
Plugin parser: RegAmCacheProgram – implemented in src/anssi_ogre_plugin/registry/amcache_program.py

Timeline data

The timeline displays the following elements:

Timeline field Value Description
Description name Program name as stored in the AmCache entry.
Additional Description version Program version reported by the installer.
publisher Name of the software publisher.
install_dir Directory where the program was installed.
key_path Full registry key path of the AmCache entry.
Related User key_security.owner_sid SID of the user who owns the registry key.

High‑level description

The Antifishing Files plugin parses the Internet Explorer anti‑phishing registry hive under each user’s HKCU hive.

  • Extracts the UserFile value and the key’s security descriptor, exposing the file used for IE’s anti‑phishing feature and the owning user.

File: configuration/registry/antifishing_file.xml
Plugin parser: RegAntifishingFile – implemented in src/anssi_ogre_plugin/registry/antifishing_file.py

Timeline data

The timeline display the following elements:

Timeline field Value Description
Description user_file Path of the file configured for IE anti‑phishing.
Additional Description order_index
Related User key_security.owner_sid SID of the user who owns the registry key.

High‑level description

The Application Compatibility Cache plug‑in extracts entries from the AppCompatCache value stored in the Windows System hive. It parses binary cache data, emitting each cached executable’s path, last‑write time, and associated registry metadata.

  • Provides the original file path used by the system for compatibility checks.
  • Shows when the cache entry was last updated, helping to infer program execution timelines.

File: configuration/app_compat_cache.xml
Plugin parser: RegAppCompatCache – implemented in src/anssi_ogre_plugin/registry/app_compat_cache.py

Timeline data

The timeline display the following elements:

Timeline field Value Description
Description path The cached executable’s full file path.
Related User key_security.owner_sid SID of the user who owns the registry key.

High‑level description

The Bam/Dam plug‑in extracts Activity Moderator (BAM/DAM) records from the Windows System hive. It parses user‑specific settings to retrieve the executable path and execution time.

File: configuration/registry/bamdam.xml
Plugin parser: RegBamDam (src/anssi_ogre_plugin/registry/bamdam.py)

Timeline data

The timeline displays the following elements:

Timeline field Value Description
Description exec_path Path of the executable that performed the activity.
Related User key_security.owner_sid SID of the user who owns the registry key.

High‑level description

The System Certificates plugin parses the Windows Software registry hive to collect system‑wide X.509 certificates stored under Microsoft certificate stores. It reads the binary certificate blobs, decodes them, and extracts key certificate attributes.

  • Provides complete certificate details (subject, issuer, validity period, public‑key algorithm, fingerprint).

File: configuration/registry/certificates_software.xml
Plugin parser: RegSystemCertificates – implemented in src/anssi_ogre_plugin/registry/certificates.py

Timeline data

The timeline displays the following elements:

Timeline field Value Description
Description subject Subject distinguished name of the certificate
Additional Description issuer Issuer distinguished name of the certificate
Related User key_security.owner_sid SID of the user who owns the registry key.

High‑level description

The User Certificates plug‑in extracts X.509 certificates stored in a user’s NTUser.dat registry hive. It parses certificate blobs, decodes DER data, and outputs detailed certificate fields together with the originating registry key information.

  • Retrieves certificate subject, issuer, validity dates, public key algorithm and fingerprint, etc.

File: configuration/registry/certificates_users.xml
Plugin parser: RegUserCertificates (implemented in src/anssi_ogre_plugin/registry/certificates.py)

Timeline data

The timeline display the following elements:

Timeline field Value Description
Description subject Subject distinguished name of the X.509 certificate.
Additional Description issuer Issuer distinguished name of the X.509 certificate.
Related User key_security.owner_sid SID of the user who owns the registry key.

High‑level description

The ClassId Software plug‑in extracts every CLSID registration stored in the machine‑wide Software hive.

  • Reveals which COM objects are registered system‑wide.
  • Shows the executable(s) implementing each CLSID, useful for locating potentially malicious components.
  • Lists “TreatAs” links that redirect one CLSID to another.

File: configuration/registry/clsid_software.xml
Plugin parser: RegClsIdSoftware – implemented in src/anssi_ogre_plugin/registry/clsid.py

Timeline data

The timeline displays the following elements:

Timeline field Value Description
Description description Human‑readable description of the CLSID.
Additional Description executable Path(s) to the executable(s) implementing the CLSID (InprocServer32, LocalServer32, etc.).
treat_as CLSID that this entry is treated as (redirect).
Related User key_security.owner_sid SID of the user who owns the registry key.

High level description

The ClassId User plug‑in enumerates every user‑specific COM class identifier (CLSID) stored in the UsrClass registry hive.

  • Provides a complete list of per‑user COM registrations.
  • Captures the executable that implements the COM object.
  • Records any alternate CLSID mapping via “TreatAs”.

File: configuration/registry/clsid_users.xml
Plugin parser: RegClsIdIUser (implemented in src/anssi_ogre_plugin/registry/clsid.py)

Timeline data

The timeline displays the following elements:

Timeline field Value Description
Description description Human‑readable CLSID description.
Additional Description executable Executable path linked to the CLSID.
treat_as Alternate CLSID this entry maps to.
Related User key_security.owner_sid SID of the user who owns the registry key.

High‑level description

The HiveKey plug‑in extracts detailed information from Windows Registry hive files. It reads each key, its path, associated values, and security descriptors,

  • Retrieves key names, full paths, and timestamps.
  • Retrieves every assiociated values.
  • Includes ownership information (owner SID) from key security descriptors.

File: configuration/hive.xml
Plugin parser: HiveKey (implemented in src/anssi_ogre_plugin/hive.py)

Timeline data

The timeline displays the following elements:

Timeline field Value Description
Description path Registry key full path.
Related user descriptor.owner_sid SID of the user owning the key.

### Fields

Output Name Data Type Qualifier Description
name String KEY_NAME
path String KEY_PATH
mtime DateTime DATE_MODIFICATION
[values].name String VALUE_NAME
[values].data String
[values].type String
[values].size Int
[values].error String
descriptor.owner_sid String USER_SID
descriptor.group_sid String
descriptor[control_flags] String
descriptor.sacl_ace.ace_type String
descriptor.sacl_ace.ace_flags String
descriptor.sacl_ace[rights] String
descriptor.sacl_ace.account_sid String
descriptor.dacl_ace.ace_type String
descriptor.dacl_ace[ace_flags] String
descriptor.dacl_ace[rights] String
descriptor.dacl_ace.account_sid String

High‑level description

The Mass Storage System plug‑in extracts information about every USB (or other) mass‑storage device ever connected to a Windows system, using data from the SYSTEM hive. It parses several registry locations (USBSTOR, DeviceClasses, USB, MountedDevices) and consolidates the results into a unified device view.

  • Provides device identification (class ID, instance ID, vendor/product IDs).
  • Lists registry paths, friendly names, and owning users

File: configuration/registry/mass_storage_system.xml
Plugin parser: RegMassStorageSystem – implemented in src/anssi_ogre_plugin/registry/mass_storage.py

Timeline data

The timeline displays the following elements:

Timeline field Value Description
Description class_id Device class identifier, encoding type, vendor, product and optional revision.
instance_id Unique instance identifier for the specific device instance.
Related user users Security‑identifier(s) of the owners of the registry keys where the information was found.

High‑level description

The Mui Cache plug‑in extracts entries from the per‑user MUI cache stored in the Registry hive.

  • Provides executable name and its human‑readable description.

File: configuration/registry/mui_cache.xml
Plugin parser: RegMuiCache (implemented in src/anssi_ogre_plugin/registry/mui_cache.py)

Timeline data

The timeline display the following elements:

Timeline field Value Description
Description executable Name of the executable associated with the MUI cache entry.
Additional Description description Combined friendly application name and company (e.g., “Company – AppName”).
Related User key_security.owner_sid SID of the user who owns the registry key.

High‑level description

The Network Configuration plug‑in extracts network settings from the Windows System hive.

  • Shows static IP assignments when DHCP is disabled.
  • Provides DHCP‑derived addresses and DNS when enabled.

File: configuration/registry/network_configuration.xml
Plugin parser: RegNetworkConfig – implemented in src/anssi_ogre_plugin/registry/network_configuration.py

Timeline data

The timeline displays the following elements:

Timeline field Value Description
Description ip_address IP address of the network interface.
dns_suffix DNS suffix associated with the interface.
dhcp Boolean indicating whether DHCP is enabled.
Additional Description network_mask Subnet mask of the interface.
gateway Default gateway address.
Related User key_security.owner_sid SID of the user who owns the registry key.

High‑level description

The Pending File Rename plug‑in extracts entries from the PendingFileRenameOperations value in the System hive. It lists file rename operations that Windows will perform on the next reboot, capturing the original and target paths.

  • Shows which files are scheduled for rename at startup.

File: configuration/registry/pending_file_rename.xml
Plugin parser: RegPendingRename (implemented in src/anssi_ogre_plugin/registry/pending_file_rename.py)

Timeline data

The timeline displays the following elements:

Timeline field Value Description
Description old_name The original file name/path scheduled for rename at next reboot.
Additional Description new_name The new file name/path that will replace the old one after reboot.
Related User key_security.owner_sid SID of the user who owns the registry key.

High‑level description

The Recent App plug‑in extracts information about applications and files recently accessed by a user from the RecentApps registry hive under HKCU. It parses each RecentApps key and its RecentItems sub‑key.

  • Provides per‑application identifiers and launch statistics.
  • Captures timestamps of the last access for both the application and individual files.
  • Includes the full command line (path + arguments) used to launch each recent file.

File: configuration/registry/recent_app.xml
Plugin parser: RegRecentApp (implemented in src/anssi_ogre_plugin/registry/recent_app.py)

Timeline data

The timeline displays the following elements:

Timeline field Value Description
Description path Full path (including arguments) of the recently accessed file or application.
Additional Description launch_count Number of times the application has been launched.
display_name Human‑readable name of the application or file.
Related User key_security.owner_sid SID of the user who owns the registry key.

High‑level description

The Run Mru plug‑in** extracts entries from the RunMRU registry key, which stores commands typed in the Windows + R dialog.

  • Captures every command a user manually launched via the Run dialog.
  • Preserves the alphabetical index indicating the order of entry.

File: configuration/registry/run_mru.xml
Plugin parser: RegRunMru (implemented in src/anssi_ogre_plugin/registry/run_mru.py)

Timeline data

The timeline displays the following elements:

Timeline field Value Description
Description executable Command line entered in the Run dialog.
Additional Description index Alphabetical index (a, b, …) indicating entry order.
Related User key_security.owner_sid SID of the user who owns the registry key.

High‑level description

The RegScheduledTask plug‑in extracts metadata for Windows scheduled tasks stored in the Software registry hive.

  • Retrieves task identifiers, authors, creation/registration dates and version.
  • Decodes action blocks (command, arguments, COM data) and links to boot, logon, maintenance and plain sub‑keys.
  • Extracts security information, including owner SID and ACLs.

File: configuration/registry/scheduled_task.xml
Plugin parser: RegScheduledTask (implemented in src/anssi_ogre_plugin/registry/scheduled_task.py)

Timeline data

The timeline displays the following elements:

Timeline field Value Description
Description task The task’s name or path as stored in the registry.
Additional Description actions.action_type Type of scheduled‑task action (e.g., Exec, ComHandler).
actions.exec_command Command line executed for Exec actions.
actions.exec_arguments Arguments passed to the executed command.
actions.com_data Data string associated with COM‑handler actions.
Related User key_security.owner_sid SID of the user who owns the registry key.

High‑level description

The Services‑Control‑Set plug‑in extracts all service definitions from a Windows System hive’s control‑set keys.

  • Provides service identifiers and display names.
  • Shows how the service is configured (type, start type, image path).
  • Reveals the account (user or system) that owns / runs the service.
  • Supplies raw values useful for correlation with other artefacts.

File: configuration/registry/service_control_set.xml
Plugin parser: RegServicesControlSet (implemented in src/anssi_ogre_plugin/registry/services.py)

Timeline data

The timeline displays the following elements:

Timeline field Value Description
Description name Internal service name (registry key).
display_name Human‑readable service name.
Additional Description service_type Service type (e.g., own, share, kernel).
start_type Service start mode (auto, manual, disabled, etc.).
image_path Full path to the service executable.
run_as Account under which the service runs.
Related User key_security.owner_sid SID of the user who owns the registry key.

High‑level description

The First‑Boot Services plug‑in provides a structured view of service configuration as it existed at the system’s first boot, useful for forensic timelines and correlation with later service changes.

  • Service name, display name and description.
  • Service type, start type and original command‑line path.
  • Additional fields such as group, tag, dependencies and driver‑specific data.

File: configuration/registry/services_first_boot.xml
Plugin parser: RegServicesFistBoot (implemented in src/anssi_ogre_plugin/registry/services.py)

Timeline data

The timeline displays the following elements:

Timeline field Value Description
Description name Service name.
Additional Description service_type Human‑readable type of the service (e.g., Kernel driver, Service WIN32 own process).
start_type Service start type (Boot, System, Auto, Ondemand, Disabled).
orgpath Original command‑line path for the service.
Related User key_security.owner_sid SID of the user who owns the registry key.

High‑level description

The Shell‑Bag plug‑in extracts the contents of Shell Bag structures stored in the UsrClass registry hive. These structures record directories and items that a user has browsed with Windows Explorer.

It reconstructs the hierarchical path of each bag entry, exposing the folder path, item type, name and associated registry metadata such as modification timestamps and security descriptors.

  • Retrieves browsed folder paths from the registry.
  • Identifies the item type (root folder, file entry, network location, etc.).

File: configuration/registry/shellbag.xml
Plugin parser: RegShellBag (implemented in src/anssi_ogre_plugin/registry/shellbag.py)

Timeline data

The timeline display the following elements:

Timeline field Value Description
Description path Folder path extracted from the shell bag.
Additional Description type Type of the shell item (e.g., root_folder, file_entry).
name Name of the shell item or folder.
Related user key_security.owner_sid SID of the user who owns the registry key.

High‑level description

The RegShimDb plug‑in extracts information about the Windows Application Compatibility Shim database stored in the Software hive. .

  • Lists each shim database GUID with the registry keys that reference it.
  • Provides the installation timestamp and the physical file path of the shim database.

File: configuration/registry/shim_database.xml
Plugin parser: RegShimDb (implemented in src/anssi_ogre_plugin/registry/shim_database.py)

Timeline data

The timeline displays the following elements:

Timeline field Value Description
Description target List of registry keys (Custom\*) that reference the shim GUID.
Additional Description file_path Filesystem path of the shim database (DatabasePath).
key_path Registry path of the InstalledSDB entry.
Related User key_security.owner_sid SID of the user who owns the registry key.

High‑level description

The Snapshot Exclude plug‑in reads the System hive, extracts entries that Windows marks as excluded from Volume Shadow Copy Service (VSS) and backup operations.

  • Provides a list of files/folders purposely omitted from snapshots.
  • Includes the registry key location, modification time and security owner.
  • Outputs data in a timeline‑compatible JSON‑L format for further analysis.

File: configuration/registry/snapshot_exclude.xml
Plugin parser: RegSnapExclude (implemented in src/anssi_ogre_plugin/registry/snapshot_exclude.py)

Timeline data

The timeline displays the following elements:

Timeline field Value Description
Description name Registry value name indicating the exclusion entry (e.g., “ETW”).
Additional Description file_path Path of the file or folder excluded from snapshots.
type Kind of exclusion, such as FilesNotToBackup or FilesNotToSnapshot.
Related User key_security.owner_sid SID of the user who owns the registry key.

High‑level description

The Subject interface package plugin extracts Subject Interface Package (SIP) records from the Windows SOFTWARE hive. It reads each SIP’s GUID, human‑readable name, associated DLL path, and entry‑point function name, together with key metadata.

  • Provides the SIP GUID, uniquely identifying the package.
  • Records the DLL location that implements the SIP.
  • Captures the function name used as the package’s entry point.
  • Includes the registry key owner SID for user association.

File: configuration/registry/subject_interface_package.xml
Plugin parser: RegSIPP (implemented in src/anssi_ogre_plugin/registry/subject_interface_package.py)

Timeline data

The timeline displays the following elements:

Timeline field Value Description
Description name Name of the Subject Interface Package
dll Path to the DLL implementing the SIP
function_name Entry‑point function name of the SIP
guid GUID that uniquely identifies the SIP
Related User key_security.owner_sid SID of the user who owns the registry key.

High‑level description

The UserAssist plug‑in extracts execution data stored in the NTUSER.DAT hive.

  • Reveals which programs a user has launched and how often.
  • Provides the precise last‑run time for each entry.
  • Handles both legacy (16‑byte) and newer (72‑byte) UserAssist formats, including known‑folder GUID translation.

File: configuration/user_assist.xml
Plugin parser: RegUserAssist (implemented in src/anssi_ogre_plugin/registry/user_assist.py)

Timeline data

The timeline displays the following elements:

Timeline field Value Description
Description program_name Decoded name of the program (ROT‑13 or known‑folder name).
Additional Description run Adjusted run count (raw count minus 5).
Related User key_security.owner_sid SID of the user who owns the registry key.

High‑level description

The User Profile plug‑in extracts data about Windows user profiles from the Software registry hive.

  • Lists all local user profiles present on the system.
  • Identifies which accounts have administrative privileges.
  • Shows the filesystem location of each profile directory.
  • Detects hidden or disabled accounts.
  • Provides registry key modification times and ACL details.

File: configuration/user_profile.xml
Plugin parser: RegUserProfile (implemented in src/anssi_ogre_plugin/registry/user_profile.py)

Timeline data

The timeline display the following elements:

Timeline field Value Description
Description path Filesystem path of the user’s profile directory.
Additional Description is_admin Boolean flag indicating whether the user has administrative rights.
user_name Logon name of the user account.
Related User key_security.owner_sid SID of the user who owns the registry key.
